name: Copy-on-Write MVCC
overview: TrustDB implements Copy-on-Write Multi-Version Concurrency Control with Snapshot Isolation (SI). Writers create new page versions instead of modifying pages in place, so readers never block writers and vice versa. EpochGuard-based garbage collection reclaims old versions once no active reader references them. NOTE: full SSI (write-set conflict detection / write-skew prevention) is a roadmap item; current commit path provides Snapshot Isolation only.

howItWorks.title: Snapshot Acquisition
howItWorks.description: Each transaction acquires a snapshot by recording the current root page ID. All reads follow page pointers from this root, seeing a consistent point-in-time view regardless of concurrent writes.

howItWorks.title: Copy-on-Write
howItWorks.description: When a transaction modifies a page, it copies the page, applies the modification to the copy, and updates the path from the modified page to the root. The original pages remain untouched for active readers.

howItWorks.title: Snapshot Isolation (SSI planned)
howItWorks.description: Current commit path provides Snapshot Isolation: writers see their own writes; readers see a consistent committed snapshot. SSI (write-set conflict detection / write-skew prevention) is roadmap — the structural primitives (conflict_graph, epoch_guard, partitioned_tx) ship as modules but are not yet wired into tx::transaction::commit().

howItWorks.title: EpochGuard GC
howItWorks.description: EpochGuard tracks which snapshots are still active. When the oldest active snapshot advances past a version, the old pages become eligible for reclamation. GC runs asynchronously without blocking reads or writes.

comparisons.source: Similar benefit but different implementation (in-place with dead tuples)
Readers don't block but write locks exist
In-place updates with dead tuple overhead

comparisons.narrative: CoW MVCC is a proven design used by LMDB, BoltDB, and (conceptually) ZFS/Btrfs. TrustDB's implementation currently provides Snapshot Isolation; SSI is on the roadmap. PostgreSQL's MVCC is more mature and handles more edge cases, but its VACUUM-based GC can cause performance issues that EpochGuard avoids. The main tradeoff is write amplification from path copying.

competitors.approach: In-place MVCC with dead tuple tracking and VACUUM GC
competitors.limitation: VACUUM can cause I/O spikes; bloat accumulation; more mature but operationally complex

competitors.approach: Single-writer CoW B-tree with no GC needed
competitors.limitation: Single writer eliminates GC concern but limits write concurrency

competitors.approach: MVCC on RocksDB with SSI and intent-based conflict detection
competitors.limitation: Distributed coordination overhead; more complex but handles distributed transactions
Single writer; readers see committed snapshots; no serializable isolation

evidence.label: SSI conflict detection (modules ship; not wired into commit path yet)
